figures.caption: Table 1: Evaluation results of Minecraft agents on over 800 tasks. ASR is reported as the mean value alongside its standard deviation. The best performance is marked in bold, and the second-best performance is underlined.
Figure 5: Step count histograms over ten rollouts for eight RL tasks.
Table 5: Hyperparameter settings across different training stages.
Figure 7: Case study of the agent synthesizing a Stone Shovel.
Table 4: Mapping binary primitives to the standard Minecraft controls.
Table 3: Ablation study on RL algorithms and the discount factor γ. All experiments are trained on the same task set for the same number of training steps to ensure a fair comparison.

Figure 3: Performance comparison between the PPO baseline and GROW, our proposed RL framework, across eight training tasks. The learning curves show that GROW outperforms PPO by achieving significantly higher success rates and faster convergence in most tasks, highlighting the effectiveness of GROW as an RL framework.

Figure 2: Overview of GROW, a RL framework for open-world VLM agents. Standard GRPO collects full-trajectory samples from the G rollouts, which often leads to excessively long and noisy contexts in open-world tasks. In the contrast, GROW addresses this issue by decomposing these trajectories into state-action samples, ensuring appropriate context lengths.
Figure 6: Behavioral analysis of the agent engaging a Guardian in combat.
Table 7: Hyperparameter settings for policy initialization in simulated Language Table.
